item.title: Mauritius weather bulletin – 20 March 2026
item.description: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 16h30 on Friday 20 March 2026.General situation:Clouds coming from the Eastern sector will influence the local weather at times.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Cloudy at times tonight and tomorrow morning with showers mainly over the Eastern part of the island and over the Central plateau.The minimum temperature will be between 20 and 22 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 24 and 26 degrees Celsius along the coasts.In the afternoon, few showers are expected over the highgrounds.Mainly fair elsewhere.The maximum temperature will be between 25 and 28 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 30 and 33 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.Wind will blow from the East-Southeast at about 15 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with waves of the order of 1 metre 50.Supplementary information:The equinox will take place today Friday 20 March 2026 at 18h46.The northern and southern hemispheres will receive nearly the same amount of sunlight.High tides:Tomorrow 01h58 and 14h31.Low tides:20h09 and tomorrow 08h25.Sunset:18h21.Sunrise (tomorrow):06h13.Atmospheric pressure at 16h00:1013 hectopascals. Weather news for Mauritius issued at 16h30 on Friday 20 March 2026. General situation:Clouds coming from the Eastern sector will influence the local weather at times. Forecast for the next 24 hours:Cloudy at times tonight and tomorrow morning with showers mainly over the Eastern part of the island and over the Central plateau. The minimum temperature will be between 20 and 22 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 24 and 26 degrees Celsius along the coasts. In the afternoon, few showers are expected over the highgrounds.Mainly fair elsewhere. The maximum temperature will be between 25 and 28 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 30 and 33 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions. Wind will blow from the East-Southeast at about 15 km/h. Sea moderate beyond the reefs with waves of the order of 1 metre 50. Supplementary information:The equinox will take place today Friday 20 March 2026 at 18h46.The northern and southern hemispheres will receive nearly the same amount of sunlight. High tides:Tomorrow 01h58 and 14h31.Low tides:20h09 and tomorrow 08h25. Atmospheric pressure at 16h00:1013 hectopascals. item.description: Europe is under pressure as Trump pushes allies to support the Iran war. How are they responding?

item.description: Oil and gas facilities in the Gulf have been attacked since early in the war on Iran.

item.title: Iran: The ‘Mosaic Doctrine’, Zelensky V Orban, Hungary-splaining
item.description: In a week where assassinations have continued within the top ranks of Iran’s regime, the minister of intelligence, the commander of the Bases paramilitary and the powerful security chief Ali Larijani included, but a weakened government seems to have had little bearing on Tehran’s retaliation across Israel and nine gulf states.Just one week ago, President Trump spoke of a war that was, “very complete, pretty much”, that now appears to have been premature, and over-confident. There’s no more talk

item.title: QatarEnergy CEO says warned US, industry officials against attack on energy
item.description: QatarEnergy CEO Saad al-Kaabi says he warned US of 'consequences' of attacking Iranian energy infrastructure.

item.title: In Sudan's refugee camps, Eid is overshadowed by war
item.description: Displaced Sudanese citizens gathered for Eid prayers and preparec food in Tawila camp in North Darfur, as the war in their home country weighs heavily on the celebrations.

item.title: A spiralling energy crisis: Strikes on gas, oil sites have sent prices soaring again
item.description: An analysis of the spiralling energy crisis with Mohamad Al-Sabban, energy economist and longtime advisor to the Saudi Government.

item.title: Trump: No Iranian leaders left to talk to, says war going ‘extremely well’
item.description: US President Donald Trump said that Iranian leaders are “all gone” and that the White House has “nobody left to talk to.

item.title: Sri Lanka says it denied US request to land two aircraft at Mattala airport
item.description: President Anura Kumara Dissanayake says request made on February 26, two days before US and Israel launched war on Iran.

item.title: Walker, Texas Ranger star and martial arts icon Chuck Norris dies at 86
item.description: A six-time karate champion and action film star, Norris was hospitalised in Hawaii on Thursday for unknown causes.

item.title: Iranians celebrate Persian New Year in first wartime Nowruz in decades
item.description: Residents of Tehran exchange new year's wishes as US and Israeli attacks continue through the spring equinox.

item.title: Iran: From Khamenei to Khamenei
item.description: How Iran’s power structure was built, and how it survives its architect.

item.title: Trump administration seeks billions from Harvard in anti-Semitism lawsuit
item.description: Harvard University has rejected the complaint as 'another pretextual and retaliatory action' from the Trump White House.

item.title: Explosion hits Jerusalem’s Old City after Iran missile alert
item.description: An explosion struck Jerusalem’s Old City seconds after sirens warned of an incoming Iranian missile.

item.title: Cuba ‘categorically’ rejects prospect of removing Diaz-Canel in US talks
item.description: Minister Carlos Fernandez de Cossio says he is open to US trade talks but refused to entertain a leadership shake-up.

item.title: No, Iran didn't publish a 'list' of 11 American cities to target
item.description: A video claiming that Iran published a list of 11 American cities it plans to target has gone viral since it was first posted on Instagram on March 3. We found the origin of this list, and it turns out that it has nothing to do with Iran.

item.title: 'Polish populists have decided to turn anti-European': Warsaw mayor Rafal Trzaskowski
item.description: Talking Europe travels to Warsaw to meet the mayor of the city and former presidential candidate Rafal Trzaskowski, just as the political temperature rises ahead of high-stakes parliamentary elections scheduled for next year. Trzaskowski argues that a long-standing consensus in Polish politics – being pro-European and pro-Ukraine – has been broken, as populist forces try to outdo each other in an effort to attract voters. Trzaskowski is an ally of Prime Minister Donald Tusk and was Civic Coaliti

item.title: Liverpool condemns 'dehumanising, cowardly' racist abuse of Ibrahima Konate
item.description: Liverpool defender Ibrahima Konate was the target of “vile and abhorrent” racist abuse online, the Premier League club said Friday.

item.title: Action film star Chuck Norris dies, aged 86
item.description: He was the star, taciturn lawman of the show "Walker, Texas Ranger": Chuck #Norris passed away aged 86 on March 19. The martials arts grandmaster turned actor had become the iconic, tough guy over the years.

item.title: Flights, petrol cars and cruise ships: Amsterdam bans fossil fuel ads
item.description: Cities around the world are clearing their billboards of ads for flights, cruise ships and petrol cars in an effort to reduce planet-warming gases. Amsterdam is the latest city to join the movement, becoming the first capital in the world to approve a legal ban on fossil fuel advertising. Dutch activists are now calling for restrictions to be enforced on a national level. Our Down to Earth team reports.

item.title: Rescued cheetahs find refuge in Somaliland amid trafficking crisis
item.description: Inside a guarded sanctuary on the savannah, 127 rescued cheetahs pace, stretch and play — survivors of a brutal illegal trade that is pushing the species toward the brink.

item.title: Alliance of Sahel states outraged at EU demand to free Niger's president
item.description: The European parliament voted last week to pass a resolution demanding the "immediate" release of Niger's former president Mohamed Bazoum, who was ousted in a coup in 2023 and has been detained ever since. The Alliance of Sahel states reacted with indignation.

item.title: Iran’s Khamenei says enemy ‘defeated’ in written Nowruz message
item.description: Iran’s supreme leader, president send conciliatory message to countries in the region as they mark the new year.

item.title: Africa: How the World's Muslims Celebrate Eid 2026?
item.description: [ENA] As the sun rose over a Middle East scarred by weeks of relentless conflict, the call to prayer resonated amidst the distant thunder of the front lines. Yet the region did not retreat into silence. Instead, it responded with a defiant, multi-colored symphony of tradition. Since the opening salvos weeks ago, a war many feared. But few truly expected with such speed, has reshaped the emotional and economic landscape of the region.

item.title: Myth of liberation: As war rages, trapped Iranians 'curse' the regime, the Americans & the Israelis
item.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Borzou Daragahi, award-winning international correspondent and investigative reporter. As Iran marks Nowruz, the Persian New Year, war intrudes into civilian spaces. The psychological terrain is marked less by overt anger than by fatigue, resignation, and a deepening sense of entrapment. A media blackout and communication breakdowns intensify this isolation, eroding social cohesion and amplifying uncertainty. External narratives of “liberation” fail to r

item.title: Kenya: Kenyan Sprinter Hopes to Leave World Indoor Championships a Superstar
item.description: [Capital FM] NAIROBI, Kenya, March 20, 2026 - Kenyan sprinter Mercy Oketch hopes to make an indelible mark at the ongoing World Indoor Championships in Kujawy Pormoze, Poland.

item.title: Africa: How Morocco Is Redrawing Africa's Avocado Trade Map
item.description: [Independent (Kampala)] Africa exported roughly 430,000 tonnes of avocados in 2025, but the balance of power moved north. Preliminary 2025 data show a decisive shift in volumes, logistics strategy, and market access, signalling how infrastructure and route stability now matter as much as farm output.

item.title: Israel says struck Syrian army camps after Druze 'attacked'
item.description: Israel's military said Friday it had struck Syrian army camps in response to what it called attacks against the Druze community in the southern Sweida province, with the Syrian foreign ministry calling it "an outrageous assault".

item.title: 'No obvious short-term way out' of war, French top diplomat tells Israel
item.description: Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said on Friday he did not see an obvious end to ​the conflict in the Middle East in the short-term, but that France and its allies would continue to work towards trying to find a lasting solution. "There is no obvious short-term ​way out ‌of the ongoing regional escalation, which has in some ways been ⁠unfolding since October 7, 2023. But this must in no way serve as a pretext for inaction," Barrot told reporters ‌after meeting with Israeli counterpart Gideon Sa

item.title: Zimbabwe: Senator Challenges Impact of Global Wars On Zimbabwe Fuel Costs
item.description: [263Chat] Zanu PF's Uzumba Maramba Pfungwe (UMP) Senator Jerry Gotora has questioned why fuel prices remain high in the country, arguing that existing local production and infrastructure should be helping to keep costs down.

item.title: Nasa's Artemis Moon rocket rolls back to pad for possible April launch
item.description: Nasa is preparing a mission to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

item.title: Nigeria: Ex-Rep Member Dumps PDP for ADC in Cross River
item.description: [Premium Times] The News Agency of Nigeria reports that multiple defections to the ADC are gradually reshaping Cross River's political landscape ahead of the 2027 general elections.

item.title: Iran threatens to target recreational and tourist sites worldwide
item.description: Iran threatened to target recreational and tourist sites worldwide and insisted it was still building missiles

item.title: Iran's Khamenei says enemy 'defeated' in Nowruz message
item.description: Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei on Friday said the enemies of the Islamic republic were being defeated in the war against the US and Israel in a written message for the Persian New Year, Nowruz. The message was read out on Iranian television, with Khamenei yet to appear publicly since the beginning of the war.

item.title: 'Trump could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment,' ex-adviser John Bolton says
item.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, former US national security adviser John Bolton said US President Donald Trump "could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment". Bolton also said he remains mystified by Trump's reversal on regime change in Iran and warned that the Iranian opposition is not receiving enough support.

item.title: Could Iran war trigger the next global food shock?
item.description: From factories to supermarket shelves, the Iran war is disrupting global supply chains.

item.title: Rose-Hill : De la drogue découverte après un accident de la route
item.description: Un accident de la route survenu à Rose-Hill a conduit à la découverte d’une substance suspectée d’être de la drogue. L’accident, impliquant une voiture et un scooter, a fait deux blessés, le conducteur du deux-roues et son passager. Ils ont été transportés à l’hôpital. Selon les premiers éléments de l’enquête, le conducteur de la voiture présentait des signes laissant penser qu’il était sous l’influence de substances illicites. Une fouille du véhicule a permis la saisie de cinq feuilles d’alumin

item.title: Air raid sirens blare during Eid call to prayer in Kuwait
item.description: Air raid sirens sounded across Kuwait City as Iran targeted US assets with missiles and drones.

item.title: Israel strikes Syria after Druze clashes
item.description: Syria condemned the strikes on weapons sites and government infrastructure as an "outrageous" assault on Syria's sovereignty.

item.title: Pointe-aux-Sables : Un canot volé sur la plage publique
item.description: Un cas de vol a été rapporté à Pointe-aux-Sables, où une embarcation a été dérobée sur une plage publique. Selon les informations, un canot en fibre de verre a disparu entre le 9 et le 12 mars. La valeur du bien volé est estimée à Rs 125 000. Une enquête a été ouverte pour retrouver l’embarcation et identifier le ou les auteurs de ce vol. The post Pointe-aux-Sables : Un canot volé sur la plage publique appeared first on IONNEWS.

item.title: New BTS album drops as Seoul gets ready for comeback mega-gig
item.description: K-pop megastars BTS released a new album Friday ahead of their open-air comeback concert in Seoul, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people. It will be BTS's first concert after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service.

item.title: 'Harm at a population level': World Happiness Report flags social media's negative impact
item.description: Young people have a “much lower” level of wellbeing than 15 years ago due to the effects of social media use, according to the 2026 edition of the World Happiness Report, which sees Finland maintain its position as the world’s happiest country for the ninth consecutive year.

item.title: ‘Cowards’: Trump slams NATO over lack of support in US–Israel war on Iran
item.description: Trump is calling for US allies to help guarantee shipping through the Strait of Hormuz as NATO relocates Iraq personnel.

item.title: Finland is world's happiest country ninth year in a row, UN happiness report finds
item.description: Finland maintains its top position as the world's happiest country for the ninth year running, as it scores 7.764 on a 10-point scale, according to an annual UN-sponsored report. Known for its nature, sauna culture and extensive welfare system, the country's 5.6 million inhabitants generally report a high level of trust in authorities and low levels of inequality.

item.title: Dana Lixenberg's personal, political and pared-down portraits
item.description: Her photographs of celebrated personalities, from Toni Morrison to Tupac Shakur, have become iconic images. Dana Lixenberg tells us about three decades of providing her unique perspective on the United States and the evolution of a society she first started documenting in the 1990s. As her major, monographic exhibition "American Images" opens at the Maison Européenne de la Photo in Paris, the Dutch photographer tells us about being surprised by the enduring success of her beautiful black-and-whi

item.title: Guardiola sidesteps Arsenal League Cup final rivalry as ‘world collapses’
item.description: Manchester City boss Pep Guardiola refuses to talk rivalry with Arsenal at a time when 'the world is going to collapse'.

item.title: Sudanese artists rebuild cultural life in wartime shelter
item.description: In a school-turned-shelter on the Red Sea coast, music rises above the hardships of war as displaced Sudanese artists recreate a sense of community and purpose.

item.title: What US-Israeli targets reveal about Iran war goals three weeks in
item.description: From degradation of military to efforts to foment unrest, targets show array of objectives but no single endgame.

item.title: 🔴 Live: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei issues defiant statement on Persian New Year as blasts rock Tehran
Hungary's Orbán accused of disloyalty and blackmail over Ukraine loan veto
item.description: The Hungarian leader says a pipeline carrying Russian oil through Ukraine to Hungary must be repaired before any funds can be released.

item.title: Despite decapitation, Iran’s regime endures: Does Hormuz blockade give Tehran the upper hand?
item.description: William Hilderbrandt is pleased to welcome Paul Taylor, Senior Visiting Fellow in the Europe in the World Programme and a member of the Defence/Security EUrope project. He is also a freelance columnist for The Guardian. Amid the Strait of Hormuz blockade, Mr. Taylor argues that even overwhelming force cannot guarantee safety when asymmetric tools, such as drones and decentralised command structures, remain effective. At its core, he contends that reopening the Strait is not a technical or logist

item.title: Publisher cancels horror novel's release over AI claims
item.description: Author Mia Ballard denies having used AI herself when writing the horror story Shy Girl.

item.title: Norway's crown princess breaks silence, claiming she was 'manipulated and deceived' by Epstein
item.description: Crown Princess Mette Marit tells Norwegian TV she wishes she had never met the late sex offender.

item.title: Switzerland halts US arms exports amid Iran war, citing neutrality
item.description: Move comes as Swiss government also closed its airspace to US military flights directly linked to the Iran war.

item.title: Japan's PM walks tightrope in high-stakes Washington visit
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi travelled to Washington hoping to strengthen ties with the United States. The visit turned into a delicate balancing act amid US President Donald Trump's growing pressure on allies to help the US in the Iran war. Yuka Royer discusses the visit with James Schoff, Senior Director at the Sasakawa Peace Foundation USA.

item.title: 'Not our war': Palestinians caught in the crossfire of Middle East war
item.description: Four women were killed in the occupied #WestBank following missile fire from #Iran - the first Palestinian victims of the Middle East #war since it started on February 28. For Palestinians, it is yet another conflict they find themselves caught up in, with little protection.

item.title: New WTO report: high oil, gas prices serious challenge for trade growth
item.description: The World Trade Organization warned Thursday that prolonged high oil and gas prices in 2026 would "shave off" growth from an already grim trade forecast.

item.title: US easing ban on Iranian oil would signal ‘beginning of the end of war’
item.description: The US says it is considering removing sanctions on Iranian oil stranded on tankers at sea.

item.title: Nigerian Muslims mark End of Ramadan with Eid al-Fitr prayers
item.description: Muslims in Nigeria and around the world marked the end of the fasting month of Ramadan and ushered in the holiday of Eid al-Fitr on Friday with prayers.

item.title: Europe's housing crisis: Is the EU plan up to the job?
item.description: MEPs recently approved a key report on housing, with the stated aim of securing decent, sustainable and affordable housing. They are responding to an EU-wide crisis in which property prices jumped by 15.5 percent between 2021 and 2024, according to Eurostat. That has frozen out millions of people looking to get onto the property ladder. Rental prices have continued to go up as well. Housing is a competence of member states, but there has been a push to do more at the EU level. We debate just how

item.title: Will Russian oil be the biggest winner in the US-Israel war on Iran?
item.description: The price of Russian Urals has soared in recent weeks. How Russia - and other energy producing nations - could profit.

item.title: French jihadist sentenced to life in jail over IS group genocide of Iraq's Yazidis
item.description: A court in France has sentenced jihadist Sabri Essid for his part in "the genocide perpetrated by the Islamic State group" against Iraq's Yazidi minority, in the first French trial of its kind. Essid is presumed to have been killed in 2018, but without proof of his death he was tried and convicted in absentia.

item.title: Burkina Faso’s tomato export ban raises concerns over regional supply
item.description: Burkina Faso’s decision to ban the export of fresh tomatoes is drawing attention across West Africa, with traders and consumers watching closely for possible effects on supply and prices.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine wants timeline for next round of Russia talks
item.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says 'clear dates' needed as Ukrainian negotiators prepare for discussions in US.

item.title: A cultural stroll through Paris with singer Yael Naim and comedian Philippine Delaire
item.description: This week Valérie Fayolle sits down with Yael Naim, who takes us round the Gaîté Lyrique, at the beating heart of the Paris cultural scene. She tells us about her seventh album, "Solaire", an ode to freedom, imperfection and femininity. Next up is vivacious Philippine Delaire, treading the boards of the Comédie de Paris. The actress and comedian is the star of "Daddy's Girl".

item.title: Chuck Norris, US action movie star whose toughness inspired endless memes, dies at 86
item.description: From one studio to another, Chuck Norris turned discipline into legend – a martial arts champion who became the iron-jawed face of "Walker, Texas Ranger" and a larger-than-life cultural myth. He died Thursday at 86.

item.title: Eid al-Fitr celebrations begin under shadow of war
item.description: Eid al-Fitr, which marks the end of the holy Muslim month of Ramadan, began this Friday in countries across the globe. But in many parts of the world, the celebrations are being overshadowed by war.

item.title: Who are the Gulf’s military allies, and how are they helping in Iran war?
item.description: Experts say while Gulf states need support in face of Iranian attacks, it is less clear what their allies can provide.

item.title: Iran video said to show US F-35 being hit
item.description: Iran’s semi-official Tasnim news agency has released military video said to show air defence systems hitting a US F-35.

item.title: Nowruz in Tehran: Crowded markets dispte ongoing airstrikes
item.description: Iran’s capital Tehran showed signs of cautious activity on 19 March 2026 as residents prepared for Nowruz, despite ongoing US-Israeli strikes that have disrupted daily life since 28 February.

item.title: Cameroon delays parliamentary elections, lawmakers to stay until end 2026
item.description: Cameroonian lawmakers yet again voted on Thursday to extend their mandate, according to official media.

item.title: Nowruz in London: Iranian diaspora marks spring amid grief over war in Iran
item.description: Members of the Iranian diaspora in north London gathered on 20 March to mark Nowruz, the Persian New Year, despite concern over the ongoing war in Iran.

item.title: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
item.description: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

item.title: Madagascar’s president orders polygraph for aspiring ministers
item.description: Madagascar's military leader, President Michael Randrianirina, announced Thursday that anyone applying to become a minister in the new government will undergo lie detector tests to screen for corruption—a rare and unprecedented vetting measure.

item.title: Kenya: Kenya's Finance Minister Defends Infrastructure Fund but Includes Misleading Claims
item.description: [Africa Check] Kenya's finance minister defends infrastructure fund but includes misleading claims

item.title: Africa: Haysom's Life 'Marked by an Unwavering Commitment to Justice, Peace, and Constitutionalism'
item.description: [allAfrica] Geneva -- The UN Commission on Human Rights in South Sudan has expressed its profound sorrow at the passing of Nicholas ''Fink'' Haysom, Special Representative of the Secretary-General and Head of the United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS).

item.title: Iran’s IRGC says spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini killed in US-Israeli strike
item.description: Israeli and US air attacks pound Iran as assassination campaign of country's leadership continues.

item.title: Egyptians attend prayers at a mosque to mark Eid celebrations
item.description: Egyptians attended prayers at a mosque in Cairo on Friday to mark the holiday of Eid al-Fitr, and bid farewell to the to the Islamic holy month of Ramadan.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Security concerns grow in US as funding stalls
item.description: US preparations fall behind for FIFA World Cup 2026, leading to growing concerns about security risks at events.

item.title: Ghana: Aib Commissions Team to Investigate Aircraft Accident
item.description: [Ghanaian Times] A three-member investigation team, led by the Head of Investigations at the Aircraft Accident and Incident Investigation and Prevention Bureau (AIB Ghana), Captain Paul Forjoe, has been commissioned to investigate Monday's aircraft accident at Tema Community One.

item.title: Ghana: Shea Industry Key to 24-Hour Economy
item.description: [Ghanaian Times] THE Northern Regional Minister, Mr Ali Adolf John Mburidiba, yesterday launched the Shea 2026 Conference to collaborate with Ghana's 24-Hour Economy initiative, part of the national strategy designed to boost productivity, create jobs, and drive industry growth to encourage businesses and services to operate round the clock.

item.title: Ghana: Northern Ghana Faces Rising Climate Threats - Bewda
item.description: [Ghanaian Times] A local non-profit organisation, the Belim Wusa Development Agency (BEWDA), has called for stronger collaboration between communities and government to tackle the growing impact of climate change in northern Ghana.

item.title: Nigeria: Shettima Present As Borno Witnesses Peaceful Eid Prayers Amid Tight Security
item.description: [Vanguard] The 2026 Eid el-Fitr sallah festival was observed peacefully across Maiduguri, the Borno State capital, despite the fear over the renewed bomb explosions and attacks on residents and military formations across the state.

item.title: Gold Prices Plunge 17% as Global Market Shifts Impact Local Trade
item.description: The price of gold in Mauritius has plummeted by 17% since mid-February, marking a significant correction in the precious metals market. Local importers report that while the downward trend has been developing for weeks, the momentum has accelerated sharply, with … The post Gold Prices Plunge 17% as Global Market Shifts Impact Local Trade appeared first on News Moris.

item.title: South Africa: High Court Urged to Permit Damages Claims Against Agrochemicals Giant Upl
item.description: [Daily Maverick] A group of Durban residents has urged the KwaZulu-Natal Division of the High Court to authorise a class action lawsuit against the Indian agrochemicals giant UPL for health injuries and financial damage they allegedly suffered after a toxic pesticide inferno.

item.title: Gulf crisis draws attention to African oil [Business Africa]
item.description: Unlike crude from the middle east, a lot of African oil does not have to transit vulnerable corridors such as the Strait of Hormuz, where events like war can put a stop to shipping. But years of constrained investment meant African producers cannot pump more and fast enough to cover supply gaps

item.title: Africa: Remarks By Unicef Executive Catherine Russell On World Water Day 2026 - Where Water Flows, Equality Grows
item.description: [Unicef] New York -- As delivered

item.title: UK and Nigeria agree deal to speed up deportations
item.description: Nigeria and the United Kingdom have signed a new agreement aimed at accelerating the return of Nigerians without legal status in Britain, including failed asylum seekers, visa overstayers and convicted offenders.

item.title: Curepipe Targets 500 Abandoned Plots of Land to Halt Rising Rat Infestation
item.description: Authorities in Curepipe have launched a major offensive against a growing rat infestation as the town emerges alongside Port Louis as one of the areas most affected by the pests. The escalation comes in response to a rise in leptospirosis … The post Curepipe Targets 500 Abandoned Plots of Land to Halt Rising Rat Infestation appeared first on News Moris.

item.title: Tunisian anti-racism activist sentenced to eight years in prison
item.description: A Tunisian court on Thursday sentenced anti-racism activist Saadia Mosbah to eight years in prison on financial misconduct charges, a ruling her lawyer condemned as "shocking" and politically motivated, marking the latest crackdown on civil society in the North African country.

item.title: Tanzania: Tanzania Network Pushes to Raise Legal Marriage Age for Girls to 18
item.description: [Daily News] Dar es Salaam -- THE Tanzania Ending Child Marriage Network has pledged to continue advocating for policy reforms to ensure the legal age of marriage for girls is raised to 18 years.

item.title: Nigeria: Borno Governor Warns of Possible Suicide Attacks in Maiduguri During Eid
item.description: [Premium Times] Governor Babagana Zulum disclosed this in an interview with the BBC Hausa Service, monitored in Damaturu, on Friday

item.title: NGOs in DR Congo offer street children skills and hope of a better future
item.description: Several thousand children are estimated to live on the streets in the Congolese capital, Kinshasa.

item.title: French tycoon Vincent Bolloré to stand trial in ‘African ports’ corruption case
item.description: French industrialist Vincent Bolloré will stand trial in December before the Paris criminal court in the so‑called “African ports” case.

item.title: Mexican teen dies while being held in US immigration custody
item.description: Royer Perez-Jimenez died amid a surge of migrant deaths under Donald Trump's immigration crackdown.

item.title: Ki zistwar zordi ? [20 mars 1345] La conjonction planétaire et les origines de la peste noire
item.description: Le 20 mars 1345, une conjonction rare entre Mars, Jupiter et Saturne dans le signe du Verseau a captivé l’attention des érudits de l’Université de Paris, alors considérée comme le centre intellectuel de l’Europe. Sollicités par le roi Philippe VI pour expliquer l’émergence d’une maladie foudroyante, les membres de la faculté de médecine ont conclu que cet alignement astral avait provoqué une corruption délétère de l’air par une chaleur et une humidité excessives. Selon leur rapport officiel, cet

item.title: M-PESA to stop sharing full phone numbers with merchants, banks by year-end
item.description: Safaricom, Kenya’s largest telecoms operator, plans to extend data minimisation across its mobile money service M-PESA by late 2026, expanding controls that limit the exposure of customer phone numbers in mobile money transactions.

item.title: South Africa's traditional circumcision claims dozens of young lives
item.description: At least 48 boys and young men died during recent initiation ceremonies in South Africa, among them 22-year-old Lamkelo Mtyho, who had no known health issues when he entered a registered school—but never returned.

item.title: South Africa: Fuel Runs Dry in Nelson Mandela Bay
item.description: [Scrolla] Petroleum companies have started limiting fuel in Nelson Mandela Bay, blaming the Gulf war for putting local supplies at risk. Oil companies are now charging surcharges on normal prices, from 5c per litre for petrol to around R10 per litre for paraffin.

item.title: Kenya: Nyongo Rallies Kisumu Residents Ahead of Ruto-Museveni SGR Launch
item.description: [Capital FM] Kisumu -- Kisumu Governor Prof. Anyang' Nyong'o has called on residents to turn-out in large numbers for the forthcoming visit by President William Ruto to launch the extension of the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) from Naivasha to Kisumu.

item.title: Trump makes Pearl Harbor remark in meeting with Japan's PM
item.description: President Trump compared US strikes on Iran to the deadly 1941 Japanese attack and declaration of war as part of World War Two.

item.title: Tributes pour in from across the world for South African peace advocate
item.description: Anti-apartheid activist, human rights campaigner, and constitutional lawyer, Nicholas “Fink” Haysom, died on Wednesday aged 73.

item.title: Tunisia: Tunisian Independence - Between Political Negotiations and Armed Resistance
item.description: [Tunis Afrique Presse] Tunis -- March 20, 1956 was not an ordinary date, but a decisive milestone in Tunisia's contemporary history. On that day, after 75 years of French colonial domination, Tunisia broke with a past of subjugation, following the signing in Paris of the independence protocol.

item.title: Liberia: Boakai, Senators Tight-Lipped On Conference Amid Insecurity At Liberia-Guinea Border
item.description: [FrontPageAfrica] Monrovia -- President Joseph Boakai and members of the Liberian Senate are maintaining silence over the outcome of a crucial closed door meeting held at the Capitol Building in Monrovia, as unease continues to grow over mounting tensions along the border between Liberia and Guinea.

item.title: Africa: After Centuries, TB Is Still the Bridesmaid, Never the Bride
item.description: [spotlight] The wins are there but the underlying reality remains, writes Professor Kogie Naidoo as she likens TB to a bridesmaid awaiting its turn to garner attention. The analogy is relevant given the overwhelming number of new TB infections and deaths each year globally for a disease persistently sitting in the shadow of other communicable and non-communicable diseases.

item.title: Morocco/Senegal: CAF Faces Pressure As Guinea Contests Morocco's 1976 Afcon Win
item.description: [Vanguard] The Guinea Football Federation has urged the Confederation of African Football (CAF) to review the 1976 AFCON outcome, citing Morocco's walkout and a recent ruling against Senegal.

item.title: Kenya: Kenya On Alert As Us-Iran-Israel Tensions Heighten Global Risks - CS Tuya
item.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Defence Cabinet Secretary Soipan Tuya has assured Kenyans that the country remains vigilant in the face of evolving global security threats, including developments linked to US- Iran - Israel conflict and wider Middle East instability.

item.title: Ghana: Shea Industry - a Sleeping Giant We Must Awaken
item.description: [Ghanaian Times] GHANA has long prided itself on being a nation rich in natural resources and human ingenuity. Yet, in the midst of our pursuit of gold, cocoa and oil, one invaluable sector continues to receive limited national attention, the shea industry.

item.title: Rwanda: Rwanda Expands Surgical Access with New Kibungo Teaching Hospital Spoke
item.description: [allAfrica] Rwanda is improving its surgical care with the opening of a newly renovated surgical department at Kibungo Level Two Teaching Hospital, previously known as Kibungo Provincial Referral Hospital - a milestone that brings critical services closer to the community.

item.title: Colombia: Torrential rain triggers severe flooding in Facatativá, over 1,000 affected
item.description: Torrential rain that began on 18 March triggered severe flooding in Facatativá, a town 40 km west of Bogotá, affecting more than 1,000 families, according to local authorities.

item.title: Tear gas and tension in Jerusalem as Eid prayers held outside al Aqsa
item.description: For the first time in decades, Al Aqsa is closed during Eid al Fitr, forcing Jerusalem worshippers to pray outside amid security tensions and tear gas.

item.title: Watch: Trump’s Pearl Harbor joke tests US-Japan alliance amid Iran tensions
item.description: Donald Trump joked about Pearl Harbor while urging Sanae Takaichi to expand Japan’s role in securing the Strait of Hormuz, drawing a muted response.

item.title: Paul Bérenger quitte le gouvernement
item.description: Un tournant dans le paysage politique mauricien avec l’annonce officielle du départ de Paul Bérenger de son poste de Deputy Prime Minister. Lors d’une conférence de presse, ce vendredi 20 mars à 11 h, le leader du Mouvement militant mauricien (MMM) a confirmé son retrait du gouvernement, précisant qu’il remet sa lettre officielle au président de la République dès midi. Cette décision, communiquée au préalable au Premier ministre Navin Ramgoolam lors d’un échange téléphonique la veille, marque un

item.title: African startups are ‘over-mentored, over-trained.’ Rwanda wants to fix that.
item.description: Rwanda's Ministry of ICT and Innovation launched Innovate Rwanda, a digital platform designed to connect startups, investors, and talent in the country.

item.title: Au SAJ Hospital : Un pas de plus vers des soins modernisés
item.description: Au Sir Anerood Jugnauth Hospital, une nouvelle page s’écrit pour les services de santé publique. L’unité de traumatologie et d’urgences, inaugurée le 18 mars, entrera officiellement en opération le 23 mars, marquant une avancée significative dans la prise en charge des situations critiques à Maurice. La cérémonie s’est déroulée en présence du ministre de la Santé et du bien-être, Anil Bachoo, ainsi que des députés Raviraj Beechook et Avinash Ramkalawon, du directeur r ...

item.description: Le groupe parlementaire du MMM a convoqué la presse ce vendredi après-midi au quartier général du MMM, à la rue La Poudrière, à Port-Louis. Voici l’essentiel de ce qui y a été dit. Rajesh Bhagwan, secrétaire général du MMM, Reza Uteem, président de la formation, les leaders adjoints, Ajay Gunness, Ariane Navarre-Marie, Aadil Ameer Meea, et Deven Nagalingum ont pris la parole pour sensiblement dire les mêmes choses : leur tristesse devant le fait accompli de Paul Bérenger, leu ...

item.title: Rethinking the sports economy with “Fan Lifetime Value”
item.description: As Mauritius prepares its Budget 2026 consultations, much attention is being given to traditional pillars of the economy: tourism, finance, energy, and digital innovation. Yet one emerging sector deserves a far greater strategic attention: the sports economy. The Government has already recognised this potential. Through the national sports economy strategy, Mauritius aims to position itself as a regional hub for sports events, sports-tech innovation and sports tourism, with the ambition of raisi

item.title: Bridging the Chagos gap
item.description: The 2025-26 Budget made provision for a revenue receipt of Rs 10 Billion which was supposed to be forthcoming from the Chagos deal which, however, has not materialized. *“Do not count your chicken before they are hatched”* is an old adage. The immediate consequences are well-known. It blows up the already yawning budget deficit and is bound to worsen the debt burden, both crucial indicators for Moody’s rating. It leaves the government in dire straits having to scramble to find alternative ...

item.title: Questions à… : Aslam Hossenally : «Le réseau d’égouts colonial complique certaines opérations d’assainissement»
item.description: Avec la hausse des cas de leptospirose, le marché central de Port-Louis suscite de vives inquiétudes sanitaires. Aslam Hossenally, lord-maire, détaille les mesures mises en place pour lutter contre la prolifération des rats et protéger commerçants et visiteurs. Après une fermeture temporaire pour un nettoyage en profondeur, le marché bénéficie désormais d’un protocole d’hygiène renforcé, de dératisations régulières et d’inspections strictes. La dernière opération de cette ...

item.title: Lancement de «Moris-Mada Business Connect» : Nouvelle ère de coopération économique avec la Grande île
item.description: À l’occasion de la fête de l’Indépendance, le 12 mars, la plateforme *Moris-Mada Business Connect* a été officiellement lancée à la Chambre de Commerce et d’Industrie d’Antananarivo, à Madagascar. Cet événement d’envergure a été organisé par la Chambre de Commerce et d’Industrie Maurice-Madagascar (CCIMM), en collaboration avec l’ambassade de Maurice à Madagascar et la diaspora malgache à Maurice. Le lancement a été conduit par Rojo Claudino Andrianasolo, présiden ...

item.title: Star du cinéma d’action : Mort de l'acteur américain Chuck Norris à l'âge de 86 ans
item.description: On le croyait presque invincible. L’acteur américain et champion d’arts martiaux Chuck Norris est décédé à l’âge de 86 ans, a annoncé sa famille sur son compte Facebook officiel ce vendredi 20 mars. Il avait été hospitalisé la veille après un malaise. *«C’est avec le cœur lourd que notre famille annonce le décès soudain de notre bien-aimé Chuck Norris hier matin. Bien que nous préférions garder les circonstances privées, sachez qu’il était entouré de sa famille ...

item.title: 25 ans au service des valeurs humaines : Saint Mary’s West, une «lakaz mama»
item.description: Né dans l’enceinte d’une église avec des moyens modestes, le collège Saint Mary’s West s’est imposé en 25 ans comme un modèle d’inclusion et de solidarité dans le paysage éducatif. Une messe d’action de grâce a marqué, le mardi 17 mars, ce quart de siècle d’existence placé sous le signe de la transmission de valeurs humaines fortes. La cérémonie, présidée par le cardinal Maurice Piat, s’est tenue en présence de Mgr Jean Michaël Durhône et du père Jérôme d ...

item.title: Interview : Anaistah Dabee : «Les congés menstruels pourraient réellement aider beaucoup de femmes»
item.description: Les douleurs sont parfois si intenses qu’il devient impossible de travailler. Longtemps, Anaistah Dabee a pourtant essayé de s’adapter au rythme du salariat, malgré l’endométriose qui bouleversait son quotidien. Jusqu’au jour où elle a compris qu’il lui faudrait repenser son rapport au travail. Aujourd’hui âgée de 28 ans, cette directrice créative a choisi de créer son propre espace professionnel en fondant Reklam – The Creative Agency Ltd, une agence de branding et de comm ...

item.title: Éducation : Ingérences alléguées et plaintes au Bureau du travail : un collège sous tension
item.description: Le courant semble de plus en plus difficile à passer entre certains membres du corps enseignant d’un collège de Rose-Hill et la *Private Secondary Education Authority* (PSEA). En cause : les méthodes d’intervention attribuées à un officier de cette instance, qui suscitent incompréhension et malaise au sein de l’établissement. Cette situation interpelle également l’*Union of Private Secondary Education Employees* (UPSEE), qui évoque une ingérence de plus en plus marquée dans la ...

item.title: Bharat Electricity Summit 2026 : Maurice et l’Inde renforcent leur coopération énergétique
item.description: Lors du Bharat Electricity Summit 2026 à New Delhi, la délégation mauricienne conduite par le ministre de l’Énergie, Patrick Assirvaden, a eu une réunion bilatérale avec son homologue indien, Shri Manohar Lal aujourd’hui, vendredi 20 mars. Cette rencontre s’inscrit dans le cadre du protocole d’accord signé l’an dernier en Inde entre les deux pays, lors de la visite du Premier ministre Navin Ramgoolam. Les échanges ont porté sur la coopération énergétique en cours et futu ...

item.title: Zethical remporte plusieurs SABRE Awards Africa 2026
item.description: Zethical Ltd, agence mauricienne de relations publiques basée à Port-Louis, forte de 11 années d’activité dans la gestion de crise, la communication corporate, le marketing digital et le design graphique, confirme son ascension sur la scène africaine en remportant plusieurs distinctions lors des SABRE Awards Africa 2026. Une performance qui souligne, selon l’agence, l’excellence de son approche créative et stratégique. La remise des prix a mis en lumière les succès suivants pou ...

item.title: Camp-Carol : Crise de l’eau : à sec et à bout de patience
item.description: À Camp-Carol, dans la région de Grand-Baie, la pénurie d’eau qui perdure depuis deux semaines plonge les habitants dans une situation de plus en plus difficile. Privés d’un accès régulier à l’eau potable, ils expriment leur frustration face à un quotidien bouleversé. Les tâches ménagères les plus élémentaires deviennent un véritable défi, affectant directement leur qualité de vie. Selon plusieurs résidents, le problème serait lié à des obstructions dans les canalis ...

item.title: Journée internationale de la francophonie : D'ici 2050, 9 francophones sur 10 seront africains
item.description: La date la Journée internationale de la francophonie a été choisie en référence au 20 mars 1970, date de la signature de la convention de Niamey, qui marque la création de l'Agence de coopération culturelle et technique (ACCT), devenue l’Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF). Le thème retenu cette année est *«Génération paix ? La contribution de la jeunesse à un monde plus apaisé»*. Le français est la quatrième langue la plus parlée au monde, après l'angla ...

item.title: Cours mondiaux : 17% : Dégringolade du prix de l’or
item.description: –17%. C’est la baisse enregistrée depuis mi-février à Maurice du prix de l’or. *«Le mouvement était déjà enclenché, mais là, on le ressent vraiment»*, explique un importateur, qui évoque une chute d’environ 5% à 6% ces deux derniers jours. Selon une analyse publiée par *«Forbes»*, plusieurs facteurs expliquent ce recul. La hausse des prix de l’énergie, dans un contexte de tensions géopolitiques, alimente les craintes d’inflation et pousse les banques centrales à ...

item.title: Saison 2026 : Hippisme : 60 chevaux retirés, au moins 150 nouveaux coursiers attendus
item.description: Alors que le coup d’envoi de la saison hippique 2026 – prévu le samedi 25 avril prochain – approche à grands pas, la *Horse Racing Integrity Division* (HRID) a récemment publié une liste de chevaux qui ont été mis à la retraite à l’issue de la saison 2025. item.title: Questions à… : Ali Jookhun : «Le véritable obstacle n’est pas la trisomie, mais les barrières que crée la société»
item.description: Dans le sillage de la Journée mondiale de la trisomie, célébrée le 21 mars, la question de l’inclusion et du soutien aux familles est plus que jamais au cœur des préoccupations. Dans ce contexte, Ali Jookhun, président du Conseil exécutif de l’African Down Syndrome Network, revient sur les défis à relever et les actions nécessaires pour bâtir des sociétés plus inclusives à travers le continent africain. 🔵 **Cette année, le thème met l’accent sur la solitude. item.title: Paul Bérenger démissionne de son poste de DPM : «Mo leker kase»
item.description: Dans la prolongation jouée par la presse, nous avons interrogé Paul Bérenger à propos de ses sous-entendus sur le *«gang des cinq»*, l’un des sujets de dissension entre le PM Navin Ramgoolam et lui. L’animal politique a fait dans le sarcasme : *«Ki ou le mo dir ou ? Kan mo koz li koripsion, li dir ou li pena prev. Kom si kan dimounn fer koripsion li pou dir ‘kit enn resi’ !»* Dans *«les jours à venir», comme il l’a maintes fois répété, il reviendra sur ce «dossier» ...

item.title: Répercussions du conflit au Moyen-Orient : Navin Ramgoolam prévient : «Li pou enn bidzet dir, mo bien sagrin»
item.description: Interrogé par la presse après la cérémonie de la communauté télougoue pour Ugadi, jeudi, au Mahatma Gandhi Institute, le chef du gouvernement a, entre autres abordé le contexte international tendu, marqué par le conflit au Moyen-Orient. «Ena bato pe fer letour. Savedir lesans pou monte. Nou pena swa parski nou pa prodir petrol.» Il a également prévenu, fataliste, que ce conflit aura des répercussions sur le coût pour les Mauriciens : «Li pou enn bidzet dir, mo bien sagrin.» ...

item.title: Leptospirose : Curepipe contre-attaque face à l’invasion de rats
item.description: Cité comme l’une des villes les plus touchées par la prolifération de rats, au même titre que Port-Louis, Curepipe a décidé de passer à l’offensive. Face à la recrudescence des cas de leptospirose à travers le pays, la municipalité déploie un plan d’action d’envergure mêlant dératisation massive, opérations de nettoyage et responsabilisation citoyenne. Julien Permal, conseiller municipal, souligne que cette mobilisation s’inscrit dans une stratégie globale visant à r ...

item.title: Alerte sanitaire : Laits infantiles : l’ACIM appelle à la vigilance
item.description: L’association des consommateurs de l’île Maurice (ACIM) a tenu une conférence de presse mercredi afin d’alerter le public sur une situation jugée préoccupante concernant des laits infantiles. L’association a annoncé le rappel de certains lots de laits pour nourrissons des marques Aptamil et Nursie, à la suite d’une alerte sanitaire internationale relayée par les autorités compétentes. Selon les informations disponibles, ces produits pourraient contenir une toxine appelée *«c ...

item.title: «Democracy Report 2026» : Le pays sort de la zone de recul démocratique
item.description: Dans un monde où la démocratie connaît un recul historique, Maurice apparaît comme une exception relative. C’est ce qui ressort du *Democracy Report 2026* publié par V-Dem Institute, une référence mondiale en évaluation de systèmes politiques. Ce constat intervient alors que le pays enregistre également une avancée notable sur le plan de la transparence économique : le Premier ministre, Navin Ramgoolam, avait annoncé mardi à l’Assemblée nationale que Maurice adhère désormais ...

item.title: Rodrigues : Des pépinières de coraux pour restaurer l’avenir marin
item.description: Face aux effets du changement climatique, la restauration des récifs coralliens demeure une priorité à Rodrigues. item.title: Des Mauriciens à Anfield : «Après les Turkish Delight, revanche contre Paris !»
item.description: Xavier-Luc Duval et Ruben Pavaday étaient aux premières loges pour voir Liverpool démonter Galatasaray, 4-0, mercredi à Anfield, en 8es de finale de la Ligue des champions, et manifester leur joie. item.title: Affaire Yogeshwaree Bhunjun : L’interrogatoire du Dr Arvind Ramchurn renvoyé
item.description: Nouvau rebondissement dans l’enquête sur la disparition jugée inquiétante de Yogeshwaree Bhunjun. item.title: Durcissement des conditions d’entrée : Visa US : jusqu’à 15 000 dollars de caution pour les Mauriciens
item.description: Le département d’État américain a annoncé l’extension de son programme de caution pour les visas, qui s’appliquera désormais à un total de 50 pays à compter du 2 avril. Dans ce cadre, les ressortissants de ces pays devront verser une caution comprise entre 5 000 et 15 000 dollars avant d’obtenir un visa de type B1 ou B2, destiné aux voyages d’affaires ou de tourisme aux États-Unis. Cette caution sera remboursée aux bénéficiaires qui respectent les conditions de leur visa ...

item.description: Ezekiel Aaron Cleo, âgé de quatre mois, continue de montrer des signes positifs après son transfert en Inde pour une chirurgie cardiaque vitale. item.description: Le Collège St Mary’s West a célébré ses 25 ans d’existence lors d’une cérémonie tenue le 17 mars, marquée par un appel à une éducation plus inclusive et centrée sur le développement humain. Présent à l’événement, l’évêque de Port-Louis, Mgr Jean Michaël Durhône, a salué une institution qu’il qualifie de « prophétique », rappelant que dès sa création, le collège a fait le choix d’accueillir des élèves aux profils et capacités variés, en rupture avec une vision élitiste de l’éducation catholique.
